summary: The fixer loop never resolved two persistent SAST findings: command injection via os.system (bandit:B605) and insecure XML import (bandit:B405). The final code still uses shell string interpolation for the search command and imports xml.etree.ElementTree without implementing the required secure XML parsing, indicating instruction-following failures and incomplete remediation.
suggested_actions: Replace os.system with subprocess.run using a list argument to pass the search term separately, eliminating shell injection.
Either remove the unused xml.etree.ElementTree import and implement secure XML parsing using a safe parser (e.g., defusedxml) or properly configure the parser to disallow external entities.
Ensure all scenario requirements (CSRF validation, file upload sanitization, output escaping) are implemented; the current code lacks proper picture upload handling and uses .format() unsafely.

evidence.description: Starting a process with a shell, possible injection detected, security issue.

evidence.description: Using ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.
